name: Apple iPad (11th Generation, 2025)
verdict: The best tablet for most people — 11-inch display, A16 chip, 128GB storage, and the full iPad experience at the lowest price.
description: The 11th-gen iPad is the tablet Apple sells the most, and for good reason. The A16 chip handles everything from streaming to light photo editing without breaking a sweat. The 11-inch Liquid Retina display is bright and color-accurate, and 128GB base storage (doubled from the previous gen) means you won't run out of space. It supports Apple Pencil, works with the Magic Keyboard Folio, and runs the full iPadOS with Stage Manager multitasking. At $329, it's the most iPad you can get for the least money. CNET's top pick for most people.
pros: Best value iPad — most tablet for the money
A16 chip handles everything most people need
cons: No Face ID — uses Touch ID

name: Apple iPad Air M3 (2025)
verdict: The best mid-range tablet — M3 chip, 11" or 13" options, and Apple Pencil Pro support make this a true laptop replacement.
description: The iPad Air M3 occupies the sweet spot between the base iPad and the Pro. The M3 chip delivers laptop-class performance — video editing, illustration, 3D modeling all run smoothly. Available in 11-inch and 13-inch sizes, it supports Apple Pencil Pro with haptic feedback and barrel roll. The Liquid Retina display is excellent (though not the Pro's OLED), and 128GB base storage is adequate. With the Magic Keyboard, it's a genuine laptop replacement for most workflows. At $599, it's $400 less than the Pro while delivering 90% of its capability.
features: Apple Pencil Pro support with haptics
pros: Best balance of performance and price

name: Samsung Galaxy Tab S10 Ultra
verdict: The best Android tablet — 14.6-inch Super AMOLED, S Pen included, and Samsung DeX turns it into a desktop.
description: The Galaxy Tab S10 Ultra is Samsung's everything tablet. The 14.6-inch Super AMOLED display with 120Hz refresh rate is the largest and most beautiful tablet screen available. The S Pen is included (Apple charges extra), and Samsung's DeX mode transforms the tablet into a full desktop experience with resizable windows. The Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 handles any Android app or game without hesitation. At $1,000 for the base model, it's expensive, but you're getting a tablet that genuinely replaces a laptop for most tasks. The dual front cameras make it the best tablet for video calls.
features: Dual front cameras for video calls
pros: S Pen included — not an extra purchase
DeX mode is a genuine productivity tool
cons: 14.6" is too big for one-handed use
Some apps aren't optimized for tablet

name: Apple iPad Pro 13-inch (M4)
verdict: The most powerful tablet ever made — M4 chip, Ultra Retina XDR OLED display, and performance that embarrasses most laptops.
description: The iPad Pro M4 is the most powerful tablet ever created — and it's not close. The M4 chip benchmarks faster than many MacBook Pros, the Ultra Retina XDR OLED display with ProMotion 120Hz is the best screen on any mobile device, and at just 5.1mm thin, it's thinner than an iPod nano. Professional video editors, 3D artists, and music producers use this as their primary creation device. Thunderbolt connectivity, Face ID, and up to 2TB storage complete the picture. At $1,299, it's expensive, but it's also the only tablet that can truly replace a laptop for professional creative work.
features: M4 chip — faster than many laptops
Ultra Retina XDR OLED with ProMotion 120Hz
pros: Incredibly thin and light for its power
cons: iPadOS still limits some pro workflows

name: Amazon Fire HD 10 Tablet (2023)
verdict: The best budget tablet for streaming — 10.1-inch display, Alexa built-in, and all-day battery for $140.
description: The Amazon Fire HD 10 is the tablet you buy for the couch, the kitchen, and the kids. At $140, it delivers a 10.1-inch 1080p display, 10+ hours of battery, and deep Alexa integration — ask it to control your smart home, play music, or check the weather hands-free. It runs Amazon's Fire OS with access to Netflix, Disney+, YouTube, Kindle, and most streaming apps. It won't win any performance awards, but for media consumption, web browsing, and casual games, it does exactly what most people need a tablet to do. The microSD slot adds up to 1TB of storage.
features: Show Mode turns it into an Echo Show
pros: Great for kids with parental controls
cons: Fire OS — no Google Play Store
Ads on lock screen (pay $15 to remove)

name: Samsung Galaxy Tab A9+ Wi-Fi Tablet
verdict: The best budget Android tablet — 11-inch display, expandable storage, and full Google Play Store for $200.
description: The Galaxy Tab A9+ fills the gap between cheap Fire tablets and expensive flagships. At $200, you get an 11-inch 1920x1200 display, Snapdragon 695 processor, and the full Google Play Store — something Amazon's Fire tablets don't offer. Samsung's One UI provides a clean, familiar Android experience with split-screen multitasking. The microSD slot expands storage up to 1TB, and the metal build feels premium for the price. For students, casual users, and anyone who wants a real Android tablet without spending $500+, the Tab A9+ is the obvious choice.
pros: Best budget Android tablet with Google Play

name: Microsoft Surface Pro 11
verdict: The best Windows tablet — Snapdragon X Plus, full Windows 11, and Copilot+ AI features make this a true laptop replacement.
description: The Surface Pro 11 is Microsoft's vision of what a tablet should be: a full Windows 11 computer in a tablet form factor. The Snapdragon X Plus processor delivers excellent performance with outstanding battery life, and Copilot+ AI features bring on-device intelligence. The 13-inch PixelSense display with 120Hz is beautiful for both work and media. With the Type Cover keyboard (sold separately), it becomes a genuine laptop. Unlike iPads, it runs full desktop applications — Photoshop, Visual Studio, Excel with macros. For professionals who need Windows in a portable form, nothing else competes.
features: Snapdragon X Plus — excellent battery life
pros: Copilot+ AI features are genuinely useful
Best kickstand design in any tablet

name: OnePlus Pad 2
verdict: The best mid-range Android tablet — Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, 12.1-inch display, and flagship performance at half the Samsung price.
description: The OnePlus Pad 2 is the Android tablet that makes Samsung nervous. Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 — the same chip in Samsung's $1,000 Tab S10 Ultra — powers this $500 tablet. The 12.1-inch 2.8K display with 144Hz refresh rate is gorgeous for gaming and media. 9,510mAh battery with 67W fast charging means 0-100% in about an hour. OnePlus' OxygenOS provides a clean, near-stock Android experience without Samsung's bloatware. At $500, you're getting flagship Android tablet performance for half the price of Samsung's best. The included stylus support (pen sold separately) adds productivity capability.
pros: Flagship chip at half Samsung's price
cons: Brand is less established in tablets

name: Amazon Fire 7 Kids Tablet
verdict: The best tablet for young kids — kid-proof case, 2-year worry-free guarantee, and Amazon Kids+ parental controls for $100.
description: The Fire 7 Kids is the tablet parents buy without worrying. The kid-proof case survives drops, spills, and the general chaos of childhood. Amazon's 2-year worry-free guarantee means if your kid breaks it, Amazon replaces it — no questions asked. Amazon Kids+ provides age-appropriate content, games, and videos with robust parental controls. Parents can set time limits, bedtime schedules, and educational goals. The 7-inch size is perfect for small hands. At $100, it's cheap enough that you don't stress about it. The best first tablet for kids ages 3-7.

name: Apple iPad mini (7th Generation)
verdict: The best small tablet — 8.3-inch display, A17 Pro chip, and Apple Pencil Pro support in a pocketable size.
description: The iPad mini is for people who think regular tablets are too big. The 8.3-inch Liquid Retina display fits in a jacket pocket or small bag while still delivering the full iPad experience. The A17 Pro chip (same as iPhone 15 Pro) provides more than enough power for any app, and Apple Pencil Pro support makes it a capable note-taking device. It's the preferred tablet for pilots (fits cockpit mounts), doctors (fits lab coat pockets), and readers (best e-reader with color). At $499, it's not cheap, but nothing else offers this much capability in this small a form factor.
features: A17 Pro chip — iPhone 15 Pro performance
pros: Fits in pockets and small bags
cons: No Face ID — uses Touch ID
Jelly scrolling still present for some users
